name: GitLab Package Registry (PyPI)
license: Open-core: Community Edition (MIT), Enterprise Edition source-available/proprietary. PyPI registry is GA on the Free tier and in CE.
cost: Free — the PyPI registry ships in GitLab Free (SaaS) and self-managed Community Edition (MIT). Paid tiers add platform-wide security/compliance/SSO: Premium $29/user/mo (SaaS; $19 self-managed) and Ultimate $99/user/mo, billed annually (list pricing, mid-2026).
language: Ruby on Rails (with Go for Workhorse/Gitaly upload streaming)
perf_notes: No PyPI-specific public benchmarks. Throughput/latency are a function of the overall GitLab deployment: Puma/Rails handles the simple-API/metadata path, Workhorse streams package uploads/downloads directly to object storage to avoid buffering through Rails. GitLab publishes sizing via reference architectures (1k/3k/5k/10k/25k/50k users) rather than per-format numbers; max PyPI file size is 5 GB.
scale_notes: Scales as a feature of GitLab, not standalone. Supports HA reference architectures with horizontally scalable web/Sidekiq nodes behind a load balancer, PostgreSQL (Patroni) + Redis (Sentinel) + Gitaly + object storage; GitLab.com runs the multi-format registry at very large scale. The registry stores files in object storage and metadata in PostgreSQL, so it is not a single-node design.
summary: A PyPI (and 15+ other formats) package registry built into the GitLab DevSecOps platform, exposing a basic-auth PEP 503 simple index plus twine upload at project/group/instance scope, inheriting GitLab's RBAC/SSO/CI-CD, with on-demand forwarding to pypi.org.
extras: PyPI is one format in a unified multi-format registry (Maven, npm, NuGet, Conan 1/2, Composer, Go, Helm, Debian, Generic, Terraform modules, RubyGems, ML models) sharing one auth/storage layer
Three index scopes: project-level, group-level (aggregates packages across all projects in the group), and instance-level simple API
Deep GitLab CI/CD integration: publish/install using CI_JOB_TOKEN with username gitlab-ci-token

GitLab CI ID tokens (OIDC) can publish FROM GitLab TO pypi.org as a PyPI Trusted Publisher (now incl. self-managed instances), but the GitLab registry itself does NOT accept OIDC-minted tokens for inbound publishing — uploads still use basic auth (PAT/deploy/job tokens)

Package protection rules are GA and cover PyPI (alongside npm, Maven, Conan; core feature GA since 17.6): restrict who can push/delete packages matching a name pattern to mitigate dependency confusion. Request forwarding to pypi.org is on by default and can be disabled per-group or instance-wide

Install/publish verified beyond pip+twine: Poetry, PDM, Pipenv install from the basic-auth simple index; uv installs and publishes (uv publish); Hatch publishes via publish.index.repos and Flit via FLIT_INDEX_URL pointed at the project PyPI endpoint. uv --exclude-newer is a no-op because GitLab serves no PEP 700 upload-time metadata
Sigstore Cosign keyless signing is provided as a CI/CD tutorial (signatures+certs stored in the generic registry), not native PEP 740 attestations
Supports both MD5 and SHA256 hashes; duplicate name+version uploads are rejected with HTTP 400 (immutable by default)
Available self-hosted (Omnibus/Docker), via official Helm chart/Operator on Kubernetes, on GitLab.com SaaS, and GitLab Dedicated
